Class ElTemplate

java.lang.Object
edu.iu.util.el.ElTemplate
class ElTemplate extends Object
Parsed representation of an EL template.

Template expressions are delimited by { and }. An opening delimiter preceded by \ is treated as literal text and the escape character is removed. Braces within an expression are balanced, and braces within nested inline templates (<`...`) do not end the containing expression.

Parsing removes expressions from the retained content and records their insertion points. A parsed template may then be applied repeatedly to JSON values. Expression contexts are pushed onto an evaluation stack so that they complete in reverse order, preserving the recorded insertion points as results are inserted into the output buffer.

  • Constructor Details

    • ElTemplate

      ElTemplate(String content)
      Parses template content.
      Parameters:
      content - template content; null is treated as an empty template
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if an expression has no matching end delimiter

  • Method Details

    • apply

      void apply(jakarta.json.JsonValue result, ElContext evalContext, Deque<ElContext> evalStack)
      Applies this template to an evaluation result and schedules completion of the rendered output.

      When result is an array, the template is applied once to each element with its numeric index and first-element state available to the expression contexts. When result is a JSON object and evalContext has been marked to introspect properties (ElContext.markAsIntrospect()), the template is instead applied once per property, exposing each property's name in place of a numeric index. All other values are rendered once. A completion context is placed beneath the template expression contexts on evalStack; after those expressions complete, it stores the rendered buffer as a JSON string in evalContext.

      Parameters:
      result - JSON value to which the template is applied
      evalContext - evaluation context that receives the rendered result
      evalStack - stack on which completion and expression contexts are scheduled

    • apply

      void apply(StringBuilder buffer, boolean first, jakarta.json.JsonValue key, jakarta.json.JsonValue value, ElContext context, Deque<ElContext> evalStack)
      Appends this template's static content and schedules its expressions for evaluation.

      String and number results are inserted into buffer. Null and boolean results do not add text. JSON objects and arrays are rejected because a template expression must produce a single value.

      Parameters:
      buffer - output buffer receiving static content and expression results
      first - whether value is the first item in an iteration
      key - current iteration key or index
      value - JSON value used as the expression context
      context - parent evaluation context
      evalStack - stack on which expression contexts are scheduled
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - when an expression produces a JSON object or array
